title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: Thanks considering contribution! OEIS.R small R package, contributions size — bug reports, documentation fixes, new accessor functions, bigger changes — welcome.

title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: Please open issue first anything beyond small fix (typos, obvious bugs), can agree approach invest time .

what: A note on how this package works
title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: OEIS.R gets data talking https://oeis.org/ directly: fmt=text search endpoint sequence fields, sequence’s HTML page fields aren’t text export (cross-reference context, adjacent sequences). fixtures mocks — test suite runs live site. one important consequence: OEIS occasionally changes page markup text format, , package can silently loudly break even though line R code changed. ’re fixing bug, ’s worth checking curl xml2::read_html() current live page/response actually looks like rather assuming existing parsing logic still correct — past example (fixed v0.2.0) full CSS/HTML redesign sequence page broke every function scraping .

title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: Key dependencies ’ll want available: roxygen2 (documentation), testthat (tests), devtools.

title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: Fork branch master. Write/update code R/. exported function generally follows S3 dispatch pattern used throughout package: generic plus methods character, OEIS_ID, OEIS_internal, OEIS_xml, OEIS_sequence inputs (see e.g. R/OEIS_description.R compact example). New functions follow convention unless ’s good reason . Document roxygen2 — hand-edit files man/ NAMESPACE; ’re generated. changing roxygen comments, run: Add update tests tests/testthat/. Since tests hit live OEIS site, prefer sequence IDs unlikely change (old, well-established sequences) keep network calls minimal. Run test suite: Run full check opening PR:

title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: Follow existing style file ’re editing rather introducing new one. Notably, codebase consistently uses magrittr %>% pipe rather base R |> pipe — stick %>% package code. Keep roxygen documentation (@param, @return, @examples, @seealso) date signature behavior changes. Prefer small, focused pull requests large ones mix unrelated changes.

title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: Describe changed , reference related issue. Make sure devtools::test() devtools::check() pass locally. Update NEWS.md short bullet describing change.

title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: Please include: OEIS sequence ID used (applicable), code ran, actual output/error, expected instead. suspect OEIS changed something server-side, link relevant page snippet raw response helpful.

title: Contributing to OEIS.R
text: contributing, agree contributions licensed MIT License covers rest project.

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: -Line Encyclopedia Integer Sequences (OEIS) database 390,000 integer sequences, documented description, formulae, references, links, example code several languages. OEIS.R fetches sequence’s entry oeis.org parses structured R objects, can explored, cited, plotted without leaving R. every function vignette talks oeis.org directly (bundled fixtures), building requires internet connection, output see reflects live state OEIS build time. Please respect OEIS’s terms use avoid hammering server tight loops many sequences.

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
Getting Started with OEIS.R
Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: OEIS_search_url() builds URL free-text search encyclopedia:
code: OEIS_web_url() OEIS_web_url(license = TRUE) OEIS_search_url("Dedekind psi function")

what: Number of sequences in the encyclopedia
title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: OEIS_sequence() takes ID (string form "" followed 6 digits) downloads parses sequence’s full entry: invalid ID rejected front, network request made:
code: id <- "A001615" A001615 <- OEIS_sequence(id) class(A001615) names(A001615) OEIS_check("hello")

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
code: A001615$ID A001615$description A001615$formerly   # old M-/N-number from Sloane's printed catalogs A001615$offset A001615$keywords A001615$status A001615$url

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: every entry parseable trailing date author line — doesn’t, $date NULL rather Date:

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: $terms holds (typically 50-80) leading terms shown sequence’s page: longer runs, use sequence’s b-file, OEIS provides separately package downloads via OEIS_bfile():

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
code: length(A001615$comments) A001615$comments[1]  length(A001615$formula) A001615$formula[1:3] library(knitr) kable(data.frame(link = A001615$links[1:5]))

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: Besides sequences explicitly referenced entry’s CROSSREFS section ($cf), OEIS also shows neighboring sequences lexicographic order ($seqs_in_context) -number ($seqs_adjacent):

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: OEIS_ggplot() plots full b-file (just leading terms shown ) returns normal ggplot object can extend:
code: library(ggplot2)  OEIS_ggplot(A001615) +   geom_line() +   geom_point(size = 0.5, color = "green") +   labs(title = paste("OEIS", id, "-", A001615$description))

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: OEIS_bibtex() builds citable bibentry sequence, following citation format suggested OEIS :

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R
text: Every accessor used (OEIS_description(), OEIS_formula(), …) also works directly character ID, without building full OEIS_sequence object first — useful need one two fields want avoid extra requests OEIS_sequence() makes (crossrefs b-file, particular, cost separate request): Internally, character ID validated OEIS_ID object , fields, converted sequence’s parsed internal format (OEIS’s compact %-tagged export format). can inspect intermediate object directly:
code: OEIS_description(id) OEIS_keywords(id) internal_format <- OEIS_internal_format(id) class(internal_format) head(internal_format)

title: Getting Started with OEIS.R

what: OEIS.R: The On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences in R
title: OEIS.R: The On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences in R
text: Access sequences -Line Encyclopedia Integer Sequences (OEIS) directly R OEIS one world’s valuable mathematical resources: thousands integer sequences formulas, comments, references, code. contributed mathematical discoveries cited 6000 articles. OEIS.R brings data R structured way, making easy visualize, analyze, use mathematical hypotheses contributions OEIS. Full documentation function reference: https://enriqueph.github.io/OEIS.R/

title: OEIS.R: The On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences in R
code: # install.packages("devtools")  # if you don't have it devtools::install_github("EnriquePH/OEIS.R")

title: OEIS.R: The On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences in R
code: library(OEIS.R)  # Download the Fibonacci sequence (A000045) fib <- OEIS_sequence("A000045")  # Basic info OEIS_description(fib) OEIS_formula(fib)  # Quick base R plot plot(fib)

title: OEIS.R: The On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences in R
code: library(ggplot2)  id <- "A001615" x <- OEIS_sequence(id)  OEIS_ggplot(x) +   geom_line() +   geom_point(size = 0.5, color = "green") +   labs(title = paste("OEIS", id, "-", OEIS_description(x)))

title: OEIS.R: The On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences in R
text: Download parsing OEIS sequences (terms, description, formula, offset, etc.). Seamless integration ggplot2 visualization. Helper functions: OEIS_description() OEIS_formula() OEIS_keywords() Respects OEIS terms use (rate limiting recommended).

title: OEIS.R: The On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences in R
text: Official OEIS website OEIS Terms Use Wikipedia: OEIS OEIS Foundation

title: S3 class constructor for bfile data. — OEIS_bfile
text: S3 object class OEIS_bfile following structure: bfile_name: Name bfile. bfile_url: Url bfile OEIS server. min_n:  Minimum n position first term available values sequence. max_n:  Maximum n position last term available values sequence. comments: Text comments included bfile data: data frame including data within bfile two columns: n named sequence ID including sequence values (n).

Getting BibTeX citation from OEIS sequence — OEIS_bibtex
text: x object class OEIS_sequence

title: Getting BibTeX citation from OEIS sequence — OEIS_bibtex
text: object class bibentry citation, function populates Misc entry type, use nothing else fits, following fields: key: character string giving citation key OEIS sequence. author: name(s) author(s) (case one author, separated ). title:  -Line Encyclopedia Integer Sequences. url: Link sequence OEIS. year: year publication (, unpublished, year creation). month: month publication (, unpublished, month creation). note: Sequence description.

title: Getting BibTeX citation from OEIS sequence — OEIS_bibtex
text: Fields tags separated commas. last tag finishes comma , although necessarily. tag's name case-sensitive caps used. help bibentry class, see: utils::bibentry()

title: Getting BibTeX citation from OEIS sequence — OEIS_bibtex
text: BibTeX Format Description BibTeX AUTOMATIC OEIS CITATIONS Mathematica OEIS Package Wikipedia: BibTex Referencing Particular Sequence

title: Getting BibTeX citation from OEIS sequence — OEIS_bibtex
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ x <- OEIS_sequence("A170401") bref <- OEIS_bibtex(x) # BibTeX and citation. print(bref, style = "Bibtex") print(bref, style = "citation") } # }

OEIS.R 0.2.0
text: OEIS redesigned sequence pages (old <tt>/table-based HTML layout replaced div.section/div.sectname/div.sectbody/div.sectline markup), broke every function scraped full sequence page. release re-targets scraping new page structure fixes couple latent parsing bugs found along way. Fixed OEIS_df() (everything built : OEIS_status(), OEIS_crossrefs(), OEIS_cf(), OEIS_seqs_in_context(), OEIS_seqs_adjacent(), OEIS_xml methods OEIS_comments(), OEIS_links(), OEIS_example(), OEIS_formula(), OEIS_mathematica(), OEIS_extensions(), OEIS_author(), OEIS_date()) use OEIS’s current page markup. root cause OEIS_sequence() failing outright. Fixed OEIS_description.OEIS_xml(), OEIS_formerly.OEIS_xml(), OEIS_terms.OEIS_xml(), OEIS_offset.OEIS_xml(), OEIS_keywords.OEIS_xml(), relied <tt> elements longer exist page. Fixed bug OEIS_formerly() returned sequence’s revision number last-modified timestamp (e.g. "#400", "Jun", "2026") alongside, instead , actual M-/N-number. Fixed OEIS_total_sequences(), longer matched OEIS’s front page markup; now reads total div.dbinfo returns NA_integer_ (instead silently stale hardcoded count) page can’t parsed. Removed stringr dependency introduced work--progress fix; total-sequences count now extracted base R regular expressions, consistent rest package. .onAttach() longer prints hardcoded, stale sequence count OEIS_total_sequences() fails. Updated README.md: fixed Quick Start examples referenced functions don’t exist (OEIS_plot(), OEIS_name()), removed unsubstantiated Shiny app link, pointed Contributing section new CONTRIBUTING.md. Added CONTRIBUTING.md development setup, test, pull request guidelines. Test files talk live OEIS site now call skip_if_oeis_unreachable() first, CI runner can’t reach oeis.org (rate limiting, transient block, network) shows SKIP rather failing whole suite. GitHub Actions traffic currently getting 403 Forbidden oeis.org (Cloudflare), crashing R CMD check outright checking examples (@examples failure aborts whole check, unlike test failure). Every @examples block hits network now wrapped \dontrun{}, vignette probes oeis.org front skips evaluating (also live) code chunks ’s unreachable, instead failing build. Fixed OEIS_comments.OEIS_sequence(), returned x$example instead x$comments (copy-paste bug). Added inst/CITATION citation("OEIS.R") works, root-level CITATION.cff GitHub’s “Cite repository” button works, Citation section README.md. Added pkgdown site (_pkgdown.yml, .github/workflows/pkgdown.yml), deployed GitHub Pages gh-pages branch. Added URL: BugReports: DESCRIPTION, removed meaningless Config/Needs/website: oeis.org line (field lists R package names, URLs), fixed two pkgdown accessibility warnings README.md (relative image path pkgdown couldn’t resolve, missing alt text sticker image). Fixed mechanical lintr findings across package (long lines, inconsistent indentation, missing spaces around operators/braces, dead code, unwrapped multi-line anonymous function, stray unused . <- NULL). Added .lintr documenting two intentional style choices default lintr flags package deliberately keeps: OEIS_*-style exported names (renaming breaking API change) consistent use magrittr %>% pipe throughout (rather mixing base |> pipe). Added tests/testthat/test_offline_accessors.R, covering OEIS_*.OEIS_sequence accessor methods, OEIS_bibtex(), OEIS_ggplot(), plot.OEIS_sequence(), OEIS_ID.OEIS_bfile(), OEIS_bfile_url(), author_list(), char0toNULL() hand-built fake OEIS_sequence object. Unlike rest suite, tests need network access , keep running (keep contributing coverage) even oeis.org blocking CI traffic — caught OEIS_comments() bug .

title: OEIS.R 0.1.0.1
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package. Improved documentation Fixes OEIS_links, OEIS_mathematica
